islands.name: Andros
islands.blurb: Schist massif with real springs — the wettest, greenest Cyclade.

islands.name: Syros
islands.blurb: Exhumed blueschist — high-pressure rock brought up from a subduction zone.

islands.name: Naxos
islands.blurb: Marble-cored dome — karst storage gives it the best natural water of the group.

islands.name: Paros
islands.blurb: Famous marble, thin soils — increasingly leaning on desalination in summer.

islands.name: Mykonos
islands.blurb: Granite that weathers to permeable grus — almost no rain is retained; desalination-dependent.

islands.name: Tinos
islands.blurb: Granite with a metamorphic rim — dovecotes and terraces engineered around scarce water.

islands.name: Milos
islands.blurb: Hydrothermal volcanic island — mineral-rich, water-poor, geothermally promising.

islands.name: Santorini
islands.blurb: Caldera of a catastrophic eruption — now fully desalination-fed under tourism overload.
